title: Listening - Part 4
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).

content.audio_script:
Interviewer: Today we’re talking to someone involved in a young football coach. Thanks for joining us. 
Can you tell us how everything started?

Guest: Of course. At first, I wasn’t planning to become seriously involved. 
A friend suggested trying it, and I realised I enjoyed it much more than I expected.

Interviewer: Was it difficult in the beginning?

Guest: Yes, definitely. The biggest problem was finding enough free time. 
I had schoolwork, family activities and other responsibilities, so balancing everything wasn’t easy.

Interviewer: What surprised you most?

Guest: Honestly, I was surprised by how friendly and supportive people were. 
Everyone was willing to answer questions and help beginners improve.

Interviewer: What advice would you give to other teenagers?

Guest: I’d say it’s important to practise regularly and stay patient. 
Progress can feel slow at first, but things improve over time if you continue working.

Interviewer: Have you learned anything important from the experience?

Guest: Definitely. I’ve become much more organised and confident. 
I also learned how important teamwork and communication can be.

Interviewer: Finally, what are your plans for the future?

Guest: I’d like to continue improving my skills and maybe work on bigger projects later on.

Interviewer: Thanks for speaking with us today.

Guest: Thank you. I really enjoyed it.

options.C: Their family worked in the area.
